content.description:
The context menu is Hobson's primary interaction surface. Right-clicking any item opens a menu built by [context-menu-lib](item://2cc0a1c5-5c09-49bd-834a-eaa2d0183b6c), with sections for view management, item operations, and debugging.

## Live Example

Here is the actual context menu rendered for a Note item, built from the same code the viewport uses:

![Context Menu for a Note](item://2cc0a1c5-5c09-49bd-834a-eaa2d0183b6c?call=buildItemMenu&itemId=c0c0c0c0-0034-0000-0000-000000000000)

The menu is shown in *inert mode* — visually identical to the interactive version, but without click handlers. This is the real menu, not a screenshot or mockup.

## Sections

### Add Child

Adds a new or existing item as a child (attachment) of the right-clicked item. In spatial layouts, the child is positioned at the click coordinates.

- **New Item...** — opens the type picker, creates a new item of the chosen type
- **Existing Item...** — opens a search modal to find and attach an existing item

### View As

Lists all views registered for the item's type. The currently active view shows a checkmark. Selecting a different view switches the item's rendering.

For *wrapper views* like spatial-canvas-view, switching sets `innerView` so the wrapper knows what content view to use underneath.

If the active view exports `getViewMenuItems()`, the View As entry becomes a submenu showing the view's own options (e.g., spatial-canvas-view's layout toggles).

### View Settings

Opens a modal showing the three-level view preference hierarchy. Here is the actual panel rendered for the Note type definition:

![View Settings Panel](item://2cc0a1c5-5c09-49bd-834a-eaa2d0183b6c?call=buildViewSettingsPanel&itemId=871ae771-b9b1-4f40-8c7f-d9038bfb69c3)

The three levels, in resolution order: context (this location only) -> item (wherever it appears) -> type (all items of this type) -> system default.

### Simple Actions

- **Copy ID** — copies the item's GUID to clipboard
- **Duplicate** — creates a shallow copy with `(copy)` suffix
- **Make Root** — navigates to the item as viewport root, preserving view config
- **Export as JSON** — downloads the item as a `.json` file
- **Delete** — removes the item after confirmation

### Debug

Debug views (like Raw JSON) and system tools:

- **Debug Mode** — toggles `window.kernel.debugMode`, activates the element inspector
- **Inspector Mode** — toggles the DOM inspector overlay (requires Debug Mode)

## Architecture

The menu is built by `context-menu-lib`, a library item. [viewport-view](item://bd74da77-a459-454a-b001-48685d4b536d) acts as a thin orchestrator — it calls library functions and handles positioning.

The library exports individual section builders (`buildAddChildSubmenu`, `buildViewAsSubmenu`, `buildSimpleActions`, etc.) and a combined `buildItemMenu` for functional transclusion. Each builder takes an `api`, `itemId`, and optional `context`. When `context` is null (as in this documentation), the menu renders inert.

## Background Menu

Right-clicking empty space (no item under cursor) shows a minimal menu with Search Items (Cmd+K) and Open REPL (Ctrl+\). This is built by `buildEmptyMenu`.

## See Also

- [context-menu-lib](item://2cc0a1c5-5c09-49bd-834a-eaa2d0183b6c) — the library source
- [viewport-view](item://bd74da77-a459-454a-b001-48685d4b536d) — the viewport that hosts the menu
- [Views & Rendering](item://a0a0a0a0-d0c0-4000-8000-000000000004) — view resolution and rendering pipeline
